title: Why doesnt lilith has a core like other angels
display_title: Why doesnt lilith has a core like other angels
wikitext:
[]: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Fri Jun 17, 2016 5:02 pm Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels? If that is a trait of her kind, and only adam and his descendants have cores. Than unit 01 shouldn't have a core eider right? But it has so... how is that?

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jun 17, 2016 7:21 pm There are a few things to consider here. Not all of Adam's children have cores in a traditional sense. For instance, Armisael has no discernible core at all. Leliel's core appears as a red crust laying beneath its flat black body. Bardiel's core is implied to be the inside of the entry plug. (This isn't made obvious until Eva 2.0, but if you look closely at the end of episode #18 when Toji is being removed from the plug, we're told the 14th Angel is onscreen, and the inside of the plug is completely red. Also explains why Eva-01 does not stop until the plug is destroyed.) Ireul's core is... who the hell knows. The short of this is, an apparent absence of a discrete red orb does not necessarily mean that a core is absent. Judging from the image of Eva-01 being created from Lilith , it did not start off with a core, but one was instead surgically implanted prior to the contact experiment. This doesn't necessarily say much about Lilith, though, since the same may hold true for all Evas. In episode #17, Ritsuko talks about having a core "prepared" for Eva-03, which rather strongly implies that the Eva doesn't yet have one; rather, it will be "installed" in the unit once it's received by Nerv HQ. On the other hand... Eva-01 seems to have been grown whole from Lilith, whereas it's suggested that the other Evas are grown in multiple pieces and then somehow assembled. (Making Evas the latter way doesn't particularly make sense to me -- if Adam can be reduced to an egg/embryo, then logically Adam clones can be grown using the process of embryogenesis -- but oh well.) If so, then installing the cores of Adam-based Evas later on, when the unit is complete, could be construed as a choice, whereas installing a core into Eva-01 might have been a requirement. Complicating all of this further is the fact that Eva-01 physically does not really resemble Lilith, but instead has the physique and constitution of Adam and the other Evas. If Eva-01 is actually a true-blue Lilith clone, rather than an Adam clone that was created using Lilith's flesh as a "surrogate womb" (same way the Rei clones seem to have been created), then this would imply that Lilith's original form is not a fat egghead with putty-like consistency. Rather, Lilith must have originally looked the same as Adam, and subsequently undergone a transformation that yielded her signature stay-puffed appearance. When the MP Evas "fuse" with Lilith, they gain some of her bizarre physical properties, so we see it's definitely possible to shift from an Adam-like physical constitution (traditional flesh and blood; wounds persist in the expected way, etc.) to a Lilith-like one (flesh acquires a homogeneous white, rubbery quality) with seemingly nothing more than ATF hi-jinx. There is one possible depiction of a Lilith core in EoE, but this is, of course, subject to interpretation: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Sat Jun 18, 2016 2:45 am > Armisael has no discernible core at all. Indeed, although I always thought about his anatomy like kind of a Earthworm. i.e. the core being some ware along its body and we just don't get a clear shot. > Leliel's core appears as a red crust laying beneath its flat black body. I re watched ep 16 but cant really see it, where do you see that? > Bardiel's core is implied to be the inside of the entry plug. (This isn't made obvious until Eva 2.0 ... How is it more obvious in 2.0 than in the original ep 18? We see in booth the red insights and in fact only in ep 18 the screen says 13th angel. Or do you just mean that the red is much more clearly visible in 2.0? Reichu wrote: Judging from the image of Eva-01 being created from Lilith, it did not start off with a core, but one was instead surgically implanted prior to the contact experiment.

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jun 18, 2016 7:39 am @Armisael, keep in mind that it doesn't start out looking like a worm, but two ribbons in a double helix formation. If there's a traditional core in there, it must be incredibly tiny. :p @Leliel: Red with a glow beneath the black. @Bardiel: 2.0 actually labels the entry plug the Angel's core; episode 18 isn't anywhere near as obvious about it. Important question: what in the world was Bardiel's core BEFORE it possessed Eva-03? I forgot to mention the Adam embryo, which has no apparent core. That is quite revealing indeed. Hence I would dear to claim that apparently only the Adam lineage requiters a core. While the Lilith lineage don't.

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by Chainer3000 » Sat Jun 18, 2016 10:08 am Reichu wrote: Judging from the image of Eva-01 being created from Lilith , it did not start off with a core, but one was instead surgically implanted prior to the contact experiment.

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jun 18, 2016 1:59 pm Chainer3000 wrote: IIRC, that may have actually just been a retcon.

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jun 18, 2016 2:21 pm Chainer3000 wrote: Wow! That's a cool picture, I don't think I've ever seen that before. Where did you grab that from? Was it a screen shot from an NGE episode, or is it from some sort of collectable artwork book?

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jun 18, 2016 6:19 pm Chainer3000 wrote: Wow! That's a cool picture, I don't think I've ever seen that before. Where did you grab that from? Was it a screen shot from an NGE episode, or is it from some sort of collectable artwork book?

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Jun 18, 2016 7:00 pm Are those drafts available anywhere? That stuff is really fascinating.

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jun 18, 2016 7:08 pm BlueBasilisk : More information here .

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sun Jun 19, 2016 6:43 pm ^Thank you! Getting back to the subject of the thread, it's worth noting that Sachiel, Shamshel, Israfel, Sahaquiel, Zeruel, and Arael have a visible red orb core while Lilith, Ramiel, Sandalphon, Matarael, Ireul, Leliel, Armisael and Tabris do not. Gaghiel has one hidden in the back of its mouth and Bardiel is white goop. Adam is an ambiguous case. As Reichu pointed out, the embryonic Adam doesn't have any visible core. Adam as it was excavated by the Katsuragi expedition was laying face down in the ice and we never get to see its chest. The Giant of Light has a black sphere that certainly looks like its core, but since it's been tampered with by the research team, we don't know if you'd normally be able to see that or not. It's entirely possible that Lilith does have a core hidden somewhere within her body or that it's spread throughout her instead of being a centralized structure. The Angels are split about 50/50. Edit: Both versions of Unit 02 have magically disappearing cores too. NGE Unit 02 gets dismembered, ReUnit 02 sheds its armor twice and then blows up. You can only see the core when it's removed from the body by Nerv's UFO catcher claw and the cylindrical housing hanging out of 02 gamma's waist.

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 22, 2016 1:07 pm BlueBasilisk : Earlier, I mentioned Bardiel's core probably being the interior of the entry plug (an idea reused more obviously in 2.0), so despite being "white goop" it would have a core at least post-infection. Eva-02's "magical disappearing core" in #25' can be chalked up to the abundant in-film continuity errors. Cores shifted around and could be anywhere from solar plexus to sternal area to even asymmetrically positioned on the chest. Huge mess, really. I don't consider Adam particularly ambiguous, unless we're going to start doubting that she had eyeballs too just because those were also black. The depiction of the core there seems to be echoed in EoE, shortly before Eva-01 merges with the Spear of Longinus. If you look carefully, the core has a round dark area within it ( literally within it, but visible exteriorly). In the absence of any other explanation, I've offered up the idea of this dark void being a depiction of the Chamber of Guf. We do know for a fact that Adam's manifested during 2I, and I assume Eva-01 wasn't given "stigmata" just for show... ----- No comments from anyone regarding the "red moon"? Huh.

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Wed Jun 22, 2016 4:48 pm I'm not questioning that Adam has a core. It obviously does. I was questioning whether the core would normally on the surface and exposed like that in Adam's natural state, or if the research team might have done some landscaping to bring it out. We'll probably never see what Adam really looked like anyway, so it's kind of a moot point. The Red Moon being Lilith's core is the best explanation I've seen for that weirdness. It does look an awful lot like a core, doesn't it?

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 22, 2016 8:17 pm There's no particular reason that I can see for them to expose the core through "landscaping", since it looks like they tunneled through the spine anyway, if the bulge on Adam's back is any indication. Not to imply that there's an entry plug as we know it there, but a precursor form of the technology would be feasible. A combination spinal interruption / core-tapping implant, or such like. Interestingly, every one of Adam's "natural" (i.e., not Kaworu) children that is humanoid has an exposed thoracic core. Since the core is first and foremost a container for the soul, perhaps, in the absence of a soul at an early developmental stage, the core simply doesn't grow. This could explain some things about the Adam embryo and even the Evas. (Pity Sandalphon was always seen in silhouette.) However! An alternative presents itself if we look at the simulation bodies, which have an array of mysterious orbs in the sternal region. If we keep potential "wandering core syndrome" in mind, these hold some intriguing possibilities. Whether by accident or design, the sim bods' five orbs are arranged in precisely the same way Eva-01's core and four "corelets" are... - Simulation Body - Eva-01 I have my own creative interpretation of what's going on here, but I'm not sure I've ever heard someone else's, so I'll hold back for now.

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Wed Jun 22, 2016 9:24 pm Fascinating! I never noticed the five small orbs/holes or that Unit 01 had four small cores instead of just the two nestled against the big one. My first impression here is that perhaps the immature Evas/Angels has these little corelets in their early developmental stages, and one of them erupts into a full-size core once they reach maturity. Unit 01's core seems to grow and swell here. You'd never notice those little dots on something as small as the Adam embryo. Unit 01's are more spaced out than the ones on the simulation body, which could possibly explain how the core appears in the sternum sometimes and in the solar plexus at other times. Nerv techs would go through hell trying to keep the entry plugs aligned if the Evas could move their cores around like that at will.

[]: Re: Why doesn't lilith has a core like other angels?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 22, 2016 10:27 pm Eva-01's two additional corelets don't appear until EoE, and they seem to only exist for the purpose of accentuating the "crowning baby's head" imagery. (YUI'S CHEST IS A SYMBOLIC VULVA AND ANUS.) But hey, if the glove fits... My idea is basically the same as yours: Seeds+Evas (and Adam's Children?) start off with five "corelets", and the central one, under natural circumstances, will mature into the core as we know it. Under unnatural (e.g. lab-grown) conditions, no soul is present, so the core is never spurred into development. Hence, Gehirn/Nerv seem to have developed the practice of growing a mature core separately and implanting it via surgical means once the Eva is otherwise "complete". Perhaps it's, for whatever reason, simpler to accomplish than forcing the central corelet to mature without things getting messy. As for why Eva-01 still has the corelets while no one else seems to, perhaps, being the Test Type, they experimentally allowed her to retain them, and removed them with everyone else to prevent unforeseen complications. If the Angels started off with the corelets, they would probably be lost in the shift from humanoid to personalized form, since they seem to be vestigial* if anything. I don't think there's any real explanation for "wandering core syndrome" other than continuity errors. I mean, think about it. The core is a giant sphere, half of which is exposed, yes? Which means half of it has to be rooted in the body. A sternal location is nonsensical, because there's a sternum and ribs there . The solar plexus actually has some logic to it, since the rib cage flares wide open at that point. Anatomically, you have a big arrow pointing south of the sternum and screaming, "Insert big red ball here." (Some anatomical workarounds/specializations might be required to make the whole shebang 100% feasible , though I'm pretty sure I'm the only person on the planet who has wondered how the core affects the placement of the diaphragm and attachment of upper rectus abdominis and etc... ) Anyway, if this idea has anything to it, it would help further along the idea that Lilith is "supposed" to look the same as Adam, but something went wrong somewhere along the way. I think I did joke(?) at one point that Lilith's physical constitution was altered as a result of the trauma of her crash landing -- perhaps some sort of adaptation intended to minimize or outright negate damage to her body. For understandable reasons, this resulted in the loss of an obvious, easily broken core. (As a corollary, Mark.06 must have landed on the Moon head-first and that's why it otherwise looks like a regular Eva. :p ) * Saying as much does imply that all of these gods are fundamentally based upon something that evolved naturally, but this is probably an unavoidable conclusion anyway. I'm not going to hold it against the FAR if they created gods in their own image; they were only human, after all. :3

Anatomically, you have a big arrow pointing south of the sternum and screaming, "Insert big red ball here." (Some anatomical workarounds/specializations might be required to make the whole shebang 100% feasible , though I'm pretty sure I'm the only person on the planet who has wondered how the core affects the placement of the diaphragm and attachment of upper rectus abdominis and etc... ) Anyway, if this idea has anything to it, it would help further along the idea that Lilith is "supposed" to look the same as Adam, but something went wrong somewhere along the way. I think I did joke(?) at one point that Lilith's physical constitution was altered as a result of the trauma of her crash landing -- perhaps some sort of adaptation intended to minimize or outright negate damage to her body. For understandable reasons, this resulted in the loss of an obvious, easily broken core. (As a corollary, Mark.06 must have landed on the Moon head-first and that's why it otherwise looks like a regular Eva. :p ) * Saying as much does imply that all of these gods are fundamentally based upon something that evolved naturally, but this is probably an unavoidable conclusion anyway. I'm not going to hold it against the FAR if they created gods in their own image; they were only human, after all. :3
